word: G4841 - συμπάσχω - sympáschō - soom-pas'-kho - Suffer with

sections.paragraph:
- Original: συμπάσχω

- Transliteration: Sumpascho

- Phonetic: soom-pas'-kho

- Definition:

1. to suffer or feel pain together

2. to suffer evils (troubles, persecutions) in the like manner with another

- Origin: from G4862 and G3958 (including its alternate)

- TDNT entry: 5:925 & 7:787,798 & 1102

- Part(s) of speech: Verb

- Strong's: From G4862 and G3958 (including its alternate); to experience pain jointly or of the same kind (specifically persecution; to sympathize): - suffer with.
